<!-- GENERATED by scripts/sync-index.mjs — do not edit by hand. -->

# Architecture Decision Records

Every agent MUST read the ADRs relevant to its task before changing code. ADRs document **why**
decisions were made. Loading follows the contract in `.claude/rules/context-loading.md` and ADR-006:
always read `load: core`; read `conditional` when scope/triggers match; read `archival` only on
demand.

This index and `manifest.json` are generated from the ADR front-matter by `scripts/sync-index.mjs`.
Run `npm run governance:sync` after adding or editing an ADR; `npm run governance:check` enforces it.

## Format

```
# ADR-NNN: Title
- Status / Date / Metadata (scope, load, triggers, applies-to, supersedes)
- Context / Decision / Alternatives / Consequences / References
```

## Index

| ADR | Title | TL;DR | Status | Load |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [ADR-002](002-best-solution-principle.md) | Always the best solution, never the easiest | Best, senior-level, production-grade — no shortcuts or unfinished leftovers; fix, never silently remove a feature (removal needs the owner's consent). | accepted | core |
| [ADR-003](003-repo-single-source-of-truth.md) | Repository is the single source of truth; memory is repo-resident | All decisions/rules/memory live in the repo; agent memory sits in .claude/memory and is symlinked into the user memory dir, so there is one truth and no drift. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-004](004-verify-first-no-guessing.md) | Verify first, never guess — every claim must be provable | Every claim must be verified and provable (source/test/measurement); no guessing; unverified items are marked 'open', never asserted as fact. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-005](005-reusability-policy.md) | Reusability policy — no structural duplication, shared single sources | Reuse over copy-paste; one source for cross-cutting concerns (ts-rs types, HUD theme, command wrappers) — no duplicated structure. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-006](006-context-budget-selective-loading.md) | Context budget — TLDR index + selective on-demand loading + compact-survival | Boot loads only CLAUDE.md + TLDR indexes + core docs; full ADRs/rules load on demand via tldr/triggers/applies-to; hooks force a reload after compact. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-007](007-generated-indexes-staleness-gate.md) | Generated indexes + staleness gate | One generator builds all indexes from doc front-matter; a content-hash scanner fails CI on drift. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-008](008-quality-pipeline.md) | Unified quality pipeline (check:all), pre-commit and CI | One check:all gate (TS + Rust + governance) runs locally via pre-commit + pre-push; CI is last-line backstop and builds only final releases, never dev builds. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-009](009-dependency-policy.md) | Dependency policy — latest stable everything, verified, lockfiles committed | Latest stable of every dependency (verified vs crates.io/npm), lockfiles committed — current versions minimise known CVEs and avoid an update cycle. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-010](010-testing-strategy.md) | Testing strategy — test-first (TDD), unit tests from the first module, contracts pinned both sides | Test-first (TDD): the failing test comes before the code; every module ships unit tests; IPC contracts pinned both sides; coverage gated in check:all. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-011](011-security-by-design.md) | Security by design — hardened Tauri, validated IPC, no secret leakage | Credentials in the OS keyring and never in the frontend; least-privilege capabilities, strict CSP, validated IPC inputs, app-data-only writes, HTTPS-only. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-022](022-agent-delegation-governance.md) | Agent delegation — model selection and subagent governance inheritance | Pick the right model per task (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku), brief each subagent with a task-fit senior persona, and make it inherit + never bypass project governance. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-024](024-versioning-and-build-channels.md) | Versioning (SSOT + SemVer) and dev/release build channels | package.json is the version SSOT (synced into Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock, gated in check:all); every change bumps SemVer; releasing is a separate, instructed act. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-030](030-portable-governance-core.md) | Portable governance core — versioned, hash-pinned, updatable across template forks | SUPERSEDED by ADR-033: two layers became N ordered layers. The hash pin, drift gate and self-updating governance:update are retained there. | superseded | conditional |
| [ADR-032](032-config-layering-project-overlays.md) | Config layering — pinned core config, project overlays, explicit opt-out | Core config stays pinned; project-specific config is project-owned; eslint/knip take a project overlay; governance/opt-out.json opts a core path out of the pin. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-033](033-governance-layers-cascade.md) | Governance layers — an agnostic core, an application layer, and a publishing cascade | Governance is N ordered layers (core → app → project); a repo consumes upstream layers, may own one, and republishes the union to its own forks. | accepted | conditional |
